text:
COMPREHENSIVE GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT
Molecular Pathology & Genomic Medicine Division

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PATIENT & SPECIMEN INFORMATION
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Patient Name:        Margaret Delacroix
Date of Birth:       [DOB on file]
MRN:                 [On file]
Ordering Physician:  Gynecologic Oncology
Clinical Indication: High-grade serous carcinoma of the ovary, newly diagnosed,
                     FIGO stage IIIC (pT3c pN1 M0). Molecular profiling
                     requested for therapeutic decision-making and germline
                     risk assessment.

SPECIMEN A (TUMOR):  Formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded (FFPE) tissue;
                     peritoneal implant biopsy / omental core biopsy obtained
                     at diagnostic laparoscopy.
                     Specimen ID: SP-TUMOR-DELACROIX-001
                     Tumor Nuclei Content (by pathologist review): 65%
                     Adequacy: ADEQUATE for sequencing

SPECIMEN B (GERMLINE): Peripheral blood (EDTA), submitted concurrently
                     for constitutional variant analysis.
                     Specimen ID: SP-GERM-DELACROIX-001
                     Adequacy: ADEQUATE

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
METHODOLOGY
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Platform: Hybrid-capture next-generation sequencing panel covering 523 cancer-
relevant genes (coding exons and selected intronic/splice regions).
Sequencing performed on Illumina NovaSeq 6000.
Mean sequencing depth: 600x (tumor); 150x (germline).
Minimum reportable VAF: 5% (somatic); 20% (germline).
Additional analyses: copy number variation (CNV) by read-depth normalization;
structural variant (SV) detection by split-read and discordant-pair analysis;
homologous recombination deficiency (HRD) scoring using genome-wide SNP-based
loss-of-heterozygosity (LOH), telomeric allelic imbalance (TAI), and large-scale
state transitions (LST); tumor mutational burden (TMB); microsatellite instability
(MSI) by fragment-size analysis of 114 microsatellite loci; PD-L1 IHC performed
concurrently (22C3 pharmDx assay, reported as tumor proportion score [TPS]).

Germline variant classification follows ACMG/AMP 2015 guidelines (Richards et al.).
Somatic variant interpretation follows OncoKB and ClinVar criteria. Actionability
tiers follow the AMP/ASCO/CAP 2017 consensus framework.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
GENOMIC QUALITY METRICS
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Metric                                Value        Reference / Threshold
----------------------------------------------------------------------
Tumor purity (estimated)              65%          ≥20% required
Mean tumor depth                      612x         --
Mean germline depth                   154x         --
Fraction of bases ≥100x (tumor)       98.7%        --
TMB                                   4.2 mut/Mb   Low (<10 mut/Mb)
MSI status                            MSS          Stable
HRD score (composite)                 56           HRD-positive (≥42)
  LOH component                       31           Elevated
  TAI component                       14           Elevated
  LST component                       11           Elevated
PD-L1 TPS (22C3)                      5%           Low (1–49%)

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
GERMLINE FINDINGS  (Specimen B — Peripheral Blood)
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
*** PATHOGENIC GERMLINE VARIANT IDENTIFIED ***

Gene: BRCA1
Transcript: NM_007294.4
Variant: c.5266dupC  (p.Gln1756Profs*74)
Common Alias: 5382insC; rs80357906
Zygosity: HETEROZYGOUS (germline)
VAF (blood): 48.3%  (consistent with constitutional heterozygosity)
Classification: PATHOGENIC — Class 5 (ACMG/AMP criteria: PVS1, PS1, PM2, PP5)

Interpretation:
The c.5266dupC (5382insC) variant introduces a single-cytosine insertion in exon 20
of BRCA1, resulting in a frameshift at codon 1756 and a premature termination codon
74 amino acids downstream (p.Gln1756Profs*74). This is expected to produce a
non-functional truncated BRCA1 protein subject to nonsense-mediated mRNA decay.
This variant is one of the most prevalent BRCA1 founder mutations worldwide,
especially in populations of Ashkenazi Jewish, Eastern European (Polish, Russian),
and French-Canadian descent. It is extensively documented in the literature as
conferring a markedly elevated lifetime risk of high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma
(~44% by age 70) and breast carcinoma (~65–72% by age 70). Functional studies
consistently demonstrate abrogation of homologous recombination DNA repair.

Germline Genetic Counseling Note: This finding has implications for the patient's
first-degree biological relatives. Cascade genetic testing is recommended. The
patient should be referred to a certified genetic counselor for result disclosure
and family communication assistance. (Disclosure and counseling referral are the
responsibility of the ordering provider.)

No additional pathogenic or likely pathogenic germline variants identified in
other hereditary cancer genes (including BRCA2, PALB2, RAD51C, RAD51D, CHEK2,
ATM, MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2, EPCAM) on this panel.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SOMATIC FINDINGS  (Specimen A — Tumor, FFPE)
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Pathogenic / Likely Pathogenic Somatic Alterations:

┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Gene    │ Variant                  │ VAF    │ CN  │ Classification │ Tier    │
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ TP53    │ c.742C>T  p.Arg248Trp    │ 68.2%  │ --  │ Pathogenic     │ Tier I  │
│ BRCA1   │ LOH at 17q12 (BRCA1     │ --     │ 0   │ Pathogenic     │ Tier I  │
│         │ locus); somatic loss of  │        │     │ (biallelic     │         │
│         │ wild-type allele         │        │     │  inactivation) │         │
│ RB1     │ c.1072C>T  p.Arg358*    │ 21.7%  │ --  │ Pathogenic     │ Tier II │
│ CCNE1   │ Amplification            │ --     │ 8.2 │ Pathogenic     │ Tier II │
│ MYC     │ Gain                     │ --     │ 4.1 │ VUS/Gain       │ Tier III│
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Variants of Uncertain Significance (VUS), Somatic:
  None of independent clinical significance detected.

Notable Wild-Type / Negative Results:
  KRAS:     Wild-type (no hotspot mutations detected)
  PIK3CA:   Wild-type
  CDK12:    Wild-type (no deleterious alteration)
  NTRK1/2/3: No fusions detected
  ALK:      No rearrangements detected
  RET:      No rearrangements detected
  ERBB2:    No amplification or activating mutation
  BRAF:     Wild-type
  AKT1:     Wild-type
  PTEN:     Wild-type
  NF1:      Wild-type
  RAD51C/D: No somatic alterations

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DETAILED SOMATIC VARIANT INTERPRETATION
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TP53 p.Arg248Trp (VAF 68.2%) — Tier I
The p.R248W missense substitution in TP53 is among the most frequently documented
gain-of-function hotspot mutations in human cancer, and is the predominant TP53
alteraton in high-grade serous carcinoma of Mullerian origin. VAF of 68.2% in a
tumor with estimated 65% purity indicates near-homozygous allelic representation,
consistent with loss of the remaining wild-type allele (LOH at 17p13.1). This is
consistent with the aberrant diffuse-strong p53 overexpression pattern observed on
immunohistochemistry (reported separately in pathology report SP-PATH-001).
Absolute requirement for TP53 mutation in high-grade serous carcinoma is well-
established; this finding confirms the diagnosis. Not a PARP inhibitor sensitizer
but confirms HGSC lineage.

BRCA1 Somatic LOH (17q12) — Tier I (Biallelic BRCA1 Inactivation)
Copy number analysis demonstrates allele-specific loss of heterozygosity
encompassing the BRCA1 locus on chromosome 17q12-21. In the context of the
confirmed germline c.5266dupC pathogenic variant (heterozygous), somatic loss of
the wild-type BRCA1 allele constitutes complete biallelic inactivation of BRCA1.
This is the canonical "two-hit" mechanism of tumor suppressor gene inactivation
(Knudson model). Biallelic BRCA1 loss abrogates homologous recombination DNA double-
strand break repair, resulting in dependence on alternative (error-prone) repair
pathways including PARP1-mediated base excision repair. This provides the mechanistic
basis for synthetic lethality with PARP inhibitors. This finding, combined with the
HRD-positive genomic scar signature (HRD score 56), strongly supports PARP inhibitor
therapeutic benefit.

RB1 p.Arg358* (VAF 21.7%) — Tier II
Nonsense mutation resulting in premature truncation of the retinoblastoma protein.
RB1 loss is a recurrent secondary event in HGSC and is associated with cell cycle
deregulation and G1/S checkpoint abrogation. Subclonal VAF (21.7%) suggests this
may represent a clonal subpopulation. Not directly actionable with currently approved
agents but is noted for completeness and potential prognostic relevance. RB1 loss
has been associated with relative resistance to CDK4/6 inhibitors (not applicable
here) and may influence platinum sensitivity in some studies.

CCNE1 Amplification (Copy Number 8.2) — Tier II
High-level focal amplification of CCNE1 (cyclin E1) on chromosome 19q12. CCNE1
amplification is present in approximately 20% of HGSC and is associated with
replication stress and genomic instability. CCNE1 amplification has been associated
with relative platinum resistance in some series; however, the prognostic impact in
the setting of HRD-positive, BRCA1-mutant HGSC remains to be definitively
established. No FDA-approved targeted agent for CCNE1 amplification currently.
WEE1 inhibitors (e.g., adavosertib) and CDK2 inhibitors are under investigation for
CCNE1-amplified HGSC in clinical trials. No CCNE1-targeted therapy approved at this
time.

MYC Gain (Copy Number 4.1) — Tier III (VUS/Copy Gain)
Moderate gain at 8q24.21 (MYC locus), consistent with low-level copy gain rather
than high-level focal amplification. MYC gain is extremely common in HGSC as part
of widespread chromosomal instability. Not independently actionable. Included for
completeness.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
HOMOLOGOUS RECOMBINATION DEFICIENCY (HRD) ANALYSIS
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
HRD Score (Myriad myChoice-equivalent composite): 56
Threshold for HRD-positive designation: ≥42
Result: HRD-POSITIVE

The HRD score of 56 exceeds the validated threshold of 42 for HRD-positive
designation. The composite score reflects the cumulative "genomic scar" imparted
by long-standing BRCA1 dysfunction: elevated LOH (31), elevated telomeric allelic
imbalance (TAI = 14), and elevated large-scale state transitions (LST = 11).
HRD-positive status in conjunction with germline/somatic BRCA1 biallelic
inactivation is the strongest available predictor of benefit from PARP inhibitor
therapy in HGSC, as established in the PRIMA, SOLO-1, and PAOLA-1 clinical trials.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TUMOR MUTATIONAL BURDEN (TMB) & MICROSATELLITE STATUS
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TMB: 4.2 mutations/megabase
Interpretation: LOW (below 10 mut/Mb threshold for TMB-high designation)

MSI Status: MICROSATELLITE STABLE (MSS)
Method: Fragment-length analysis of 114 microsatellite loci; 0/114 unstable

Note: Low TMB and MSS status are typical of HGSC and do not predict benefit from
checkpoint immunotherapy (anti-PD-1/PD-L1). These results are consistent with the
homologous recombination-deficient, chromosomally unstable (but not hypermutant)
genomics characteristic of this histologic subtype.

PD-L1 TPS (22C3 pharmDx): 5% (Low positivity, 1–49% range)
Interpretation: Low PD-L1 expression. This result does not independently support
current immunotherapy indications in ovarian cancer.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ACTIONABILITY SUMMARY (AMP/ASCO/CAP Tier Framework)
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Tier I — Strong Clinical Significance (FDA-approved therapies or
          established diagnostic/prognostic significance):

  1. GERMLINE BRCA1 c.5266dupC (p.Gln1756Profs*74) — PATHOGENIC
     PARP Inhibitor Sensitivity: FDA-approved PARP inhibitors with demonstrated
     efficacy in BRCA1/2-mutant ovarian carcinoma include:
       - ola (Lynparza): FDA-approved first-line maintenance (SOLO-1 trial;
         hazard ratio for progression 0.30 vs. placebo) and treatment of
         recurrent BRCA-mutant ovarian cancer.
       - Zejula (Zejula): FDA-approved first-line maintenance (PRIMA trial;
         benefit across HRD-positive population regardless of BRCA status;
         enhanced benefit in BRCA-mutant/HRD-positive subgroup).
       - Rubraca (Rubraca): FDA-approved maintenance (ARIEL3) and treatment
         of recurrent BRCA-mutant ovarian cancer.
     Additionally, Lynparza + Avastin combination (PAOLA-1 trial) demonstrated
     significant PFS benefit in HRD-positive HGSC.
     Platinum sensitivity: BRCA1/2-deficient tumors are inherently platinum-
     sensitive due to impaired DSB repair; this supports platinum-based
     chemotherapy as backbone of first-line treatment.

  2. TP53 p.Arg248Trp (VAF 68%) — Confirms HGSC diagnosis; no directly
     targeted approved agent for TP53 gain-of-function mutations in ovarian
     cancer.

Tier II — Potential Clinical Significance (Investigational or off-label):

  3. CCNE1 Amplification (CN 8.2)
     Emerging data support investigation of WEE1 inhibitors (e.g., adavosertib)
     and CDK2 inhibitors in CCNE1-amplified HGSC. Clinical trial enrollment
     should be considered. No FDA-approved agent for this alteration.

  4. RB1 p.Arg358* (Subclonal, VAF 21.7%)
     No directly actionable approved agent. Of note in the context of clinical
     trial eligibility assessment.

Tier III — Uncertain Clinical Significance:

  5. MYC Gain (CN 4.1) — No direct therapeutic implication; noted as marker
     of chromosomal instability consistent with HGSC.

Tier IV — Benign / Likely Benign / Not Reported: No variants in this category
     warrant mention.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SUMMARY AND OVERALL INTERPRETATION
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
This comprehensive germline and somatic genomic profiling study of Ms. Delacroix's
high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma identifies the following key findings:

1. GERMLINE BRCA1 PATHOGENIC VARIANT (c.5266dupC / 5382insC): This Eastern
   European/French-Canadian founder mutation is confirmed as pathogenic per ACMG/AMP
   criteria. Constitutional heterozygosity for this frameshift variant, in the
   context of somatic loss of the wild-type allele (LOH), results in complete
   biallelic inactivation of BRCA1 in the tumor. This is the dominant oncogenic
   driver of HRD in this tumor and the primary molecular target for therapeutic
   intervention.

2. HRD-POSITIVE TUMOR (score 56): Exceeds threshold of 42. Strongly supports PARP
   inhibitor therapeutic sensitivity.

3. TP53 p.R248W: Pathognomonic somatic alteration confirming HGSC lineage.

4. CCNE1 AMPLIFICATION: Secondary event; clinical trial relevance for WEE1/CDK2
   inhibitor strategies.

5. MSS / Low TMB / Low PD-L1: Profile does not support current checkpoint
   immunotherapy indication.

This genomic profile is highly consistent with BRCA1-driven, HRD-positive, platinum-
and PARP inhibitor-sensitive high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma. Germline findings
have important hereditary implications for biological relatives.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
QUALITY ASSURANCE & ATTESTATION
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
This report was generated following successful quality control metrics on both
specimens. Variant calls were reviewed and confirmed by a molecular pathologist
board-certified in clinical molecular genetics (ABP/AMP). Germline findings reviewed
by a laboratory geneticist with ABMGG certification. This test was developed and its
performance characteristics determined by the Molecular Pathology Laboratory and has
not been cleared or approved by the FDA. The FDA has determined that such clearance or
approval is not necessary. This laboratory is certified under the Clinical Laboratory
Improvement Amendments of 1988 (CLIA) as qualified to perform high-complexity
clinical laboratory testing.

Reporting Molecular Pathologist: [Signature on file]
Molecular Genetics Laboratory Director: [Signature on file]
Report Status: FINAL
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
